_meta.name: FenceClaw Municipality Database
_meta.coverage_area: Western Wayne, Washtenaw, southeast Livingston counties, Michigan
_meta.disclaimer: Fence codes change. Always verify with the municipality before permit application. FenceClaw is a reference tool, not legal advice.

ann-arbor-city.name: City of Ann Arbor

front.notes: Max 30 inches in front open space (25–40 ft from street); up to 4 ft elsewhere in front yard

side.notes: Middle zone (25 ft beyond front open space): 6 ft max, 80% opacity limit

rear.notes: Opaque allowed; building permit required over 6 ft

setback.notes: No minimum setback; fence may be up to property line, but entire fence must be on your property

permit.notes: Fence permit ($75) required; additional building permit required over 6 ft; zoning compliance permit required in floodplains

corner_lot.notes: Visibility triangle enforced at intersections and driveways per Ch. 104

pool_barrier.notes: Follows Michigan Residential Code barrier requirements; coordinate with Building Department

materials.notes: Historic districts restrict plastic/vinyl-coated; custom masonry reviewed case-by-case by HDC

permit_office.name: Ann Arbor Planning Services
permit_office.address: 301 E. Huron St., Ann Arbor, MI 48104

ann-arbor-city.research_notes: Opacity/height info confirmed from official Ann Arbor Fence Guidelines PDF (3-3-2025) and LocalWiki Ch. 104 summary. Permit fee $75 confirmed in PDF.

ann-arbor-twp.name: Ann Arbor Charter Township

front.notes: Specific front-yard residential height not extracted from §74-600 directly. ZO §74-600 was amended by Ord 6-2024, effective July 12, 2024 — added definitions and updated location/features of fences and gates.

setback.notes: §74-595 governs setbacks on arterial roads (also amended by Ord 6-2024). §74-426 covers Requirements/Scope/Uses/Exceptions for accessory uses (also amended).

permit.notes: Township issues building permits directly (NOT Washtenaw County, NOT CACA). Building permit applications can be emailed (building@aatwp.org) or submitted at Township Office. After-hours dropbox available. Credit card payments accepted. Building Official & Zoning Administrator: Peter Pace, ppace@aatwp.org. Inspection results lookup at https://bsaonline.com/?uid=284.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

materials.notes: Not extracted from §74-600 directly. Recent Ord 6-2024 amendment added definitions pertaining to fences.

permit_office.name: Ann Arbor Charter Township — Building and Zoning Department

ann-arbor-twp.research_notes: CONFIRMED from primary township sources: (1) Ann Arbor Charter Township uses Code Chapter 74 (Zoning), §74-600 'Fence Regulations'; (2) Section 74-600 was amended by Ordinance 6-2024 (effective July 12, 2024) — added new definitions and updated location/features of fences and gates; (3) Township issues building permits directly (notable — different from Webster, Lodi, Saline Twps which use Washtenaw County); (4) Building Official & Zoning Administrator: Peter Pace; Building Assistants: Jae Elliott and Libby Greeley; (5) BS&A Online for inspection lookups (uid=284). NOT EXTRACTED FROM PRIMARY SOURCE: substantive heights, opacity, prohibited materials. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: Ann Arbor Charter Township (this record) and City of Ann Arbor (separate record above, UDC §5.27.5) are SEPARATE jurisdictions sharing ZIPs 48103, 48104, 48105. Township is to the north and east of the city, including Pontiac Trail and Plymouth Road corridors. Verify jurisdiction at Map Washtenaw GIS before quoting numbers. Recommend a 5-minute call to extract substantive heights from current §74-600 (post-Ord 6-2024).

scio-twp.name: Scio Township

ordinance.chapter: Township Code §36 (Zoning) Article relating to fences
ordinance.title: Fences

front.notes: Residential: Only ornamental-type fences allowed in required front setback or required side setback adjoining a public/private street; max 4 ft.

side.notes: Side setback adjoining public/private street limited to 4-ft ornamental. Other side yard heights not extracted from primary source.

rear.notes: Specific rear-yard residential height not extracted from primary source. Likely 6 ft consistent with regional pattern.

opacity.notes: Front/street-yard fences must be ornamental type (≤4 ft).

setback.notes: All fences shall be located entirely on the property of the owner. Adjoining property owners may jointly install a fence on common property line. No fence within a public utility easement without utility approval.

permit.notes: NOTABLE: 'A permit to construct a fence shall not be required.' Only Washtenaw jurisdiction researched that does NOT require a fence permit. Substantive height/setback rules still apply, and home improvements still require zoning compliance per township FAQ — confirm with Planning.

corner_lot.notes: Corner-lot/street-side rule: ornamental fences only in side setback adjoining public/private street (4 ft max).

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

prohibited: spikes, nails, barbed wire, or other pointed objects below 10 ft (residential)
materials.notes: No spikes, nails, barbed wire, or other pointed objects/sharp protrusions below 10 ft, except on fences enclosing farmland (where barbed wire may be permitted at any height).

permit_office.name: Scio Township — Planning and Zoning
permit_office.address: 827 N Zeeb Road, Ann Arbor, MI 48103

scio-twp.research_notes: Substantive front-yard rule and the no-permit-required rule both extracted from primary source. UNIQUE FEATURE for contractor: Scio Township does NOT require a fence permit. Significant cost/time advantage compared to Pittsfield, Ann Arbor City, and Ypsilanti Township. However, height and location rules still apply — neighbor disputes can still trigger enforcement. Planning consultant available Tuesdays/Thursdays only — book by phone or email. Scio is a General Law Township (not Charter), reorganized its zoning code into Township Code Ch 36 effective July 28, 2009.

webster-twp.name: Webster Township

ordinance.chapter: Webster Township Zoning Ordinance (adopted June 21, 2011, amended through 11/02/2023)
ordinance.title: Fences

front.notes: Not extracted.

setback.notes: Webster Township ZO defines fence as 'NOT considered a structure and shall not be subject to setback' requirements. Unusual and contractor-friendly.

permit.notes: Webster Township issues 'Certificate of Zoning Compliance' permits. Whether fences specifically require this is not extracted; recommend confirming. Building permits administered by WASHTENAW COUNTY (not Webster Township).

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: Webster Township — Planning and Zoning

webster-twp.research_notes: Webster Township ZO last reformatted 11/02/2023. Fence definition explicitly excludes fences from being 'structures' for setback purposes — unusual. Building Inspector role is filled by WASHTENAW COUNTY (not the township itself) — same dual-jurisdiction pattern as Dexter City. ZIP 48130 includes parts of City of Dexter, Dexter Township, Webster Township, and Scio Township — heavy overlap.

lodi-twp.name: Lodi Township

ordinance.chapter: Lodi Township Zoning Ordinance (effective June 20, 2019, as amended)
ordinance.title: Fences

front.notes: Not extracted.

permit.notes: Zoning Compliance Permit required ($100, mailed in with stamped SASE). Township zoning compliance required BEFORE Washtenaw County Building Department issues building permit. WCBD requires building permit for any structure over 200 sq ft. Township office hours: Tuesday–Thursday 9:30am–11:30am ONLY.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: Lodi Township — Zoning Office
permit_office.address: 3755 Pleasant Lake Road, Ann Arbor, MI 48103

lodi-twp.research_notes: Township uses ZONING COMPLIANCE PERMIT (not Building Permit) at township level — Building Permits administered by Washtenaw County Building Department. Permit fee $100 (mailed in with check). Office hours: Tue–Thu 9:30–11:30 AM only. Lodi Township ZIPs include 48103 (also Scio + AA City + AA Twp), 48108 (also Pittsfield + Saline Twps), 48118 (also Sylvan, Lima, Dexter Twps), 48176 (also Saline City, Saline Twp, York Twp).

superior-twp.name: Charter Township of Superior

ordinance.chapter: Superior Charter Township Zoning Ordinance (Fence section)
ordinance.title: Fences

front.notes: Not extracted.

rear.notes: Fences ≥6 ft trigger BUILDING permit requirement (not just zoning compliance). Implication: residential fences are commonly built up to 6 ft; for taller fences, dual permit required.

permit.notes: TIERED PERMIT SYSTEM: Fences LESS than 6 ft do NOT require a building permit but DO require Zoning Compliance review. Fences 6 ft or taller require a BUILDING permit (in addition to zoning review). Decks adjacent to dwelling always require permits + inspections + Zoning Compliance review.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: Charter Township of Superior — Building Department

superior-twp.research_notes: Two-tier permit pathway based on fence height — under 6 ft = zoning compliance review only; 6 ft and over = building permit required + zoning review. Similar tier system to Northville Township (three-tier). Superior Township sits between Ann Arbor City and Ypsilanti Township.

pittsfield-twp.name: Pittsfield Charter Township

front.notes: Residential: 3-foot ornamental fence allowed in front yard or street yard if it is a corner lot. Otherwise no front-yard fence in residential.

side.notes: Residential: 6 feet max in side yard.

rear.notes: Residential: 6 feet max in rear yard. Building permit required if fence exceeds 7 feet.

opacity.notes: Residential opacity not specified in §13.06 quick-reference table. Business/Industrial/Public Facility front or street yard fences capped at 50% opacity.

setback.notes: Fence must be located entirely on owner's property. Adjoining owners may jointly apply for a shared-line permit. If fence is within 6 inches of shared property line OR attached to neighbor's fence, neighbor agreement required. Vision clearance: 30 ft from intersection corner, no fence/wall/hedge/structure higher than 3 ft (per §12.01).

permit.notes: Fence permit required from Pittsfield Township Building Department. Residential fee: $50. Non-Residential fee: $150. Cash or check at submission, non-refundable. Building permit ALSO required if fence exceeds 7 feet. HOA approval letter required if applicable. Drainage easement crossings require separate Acknowledgment Form and drain use permit.

corner_lot.notes: Corner lots may have 3-foot ornamental fence in front or street yard. Vision clearance triangle: 30 ft from intersection corner, no fence/wall/hedge/structure higher than 3 ft.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

materials.notes: Barbs/spikes only allowed on fences over 10 ft. Electric current prohibited except on active farmland. Retaining walls over 18 inches regulated as fences.

permit_office.name: Pittsfield Charter Township — Department of Community Development / Building Department
permit_office.address: 6201 W. Michigan Avenue, Ann Arbor, MI 48108

pittsfield-twp.research_notes: All key data verified from primary township sources: §13.06 confirmed via ecode360, residential heights confirmed via the township's own fence permit application Quick Reference table, fees confirmed at $50 res / $150 non-res, exemption for fences enclosing active farmland (under 4 ft) confirmed, retaining wall >18 inches treated as fence confirmed, materials prohibitions confirmed. HOA letter requirement is structural — many Pittsfield neighborhoods are in HOAs (e.g., Stonebridge, Travis Pointe, Fox Hills) so contractor should always ask about HOA up front.

belleville-city.name: City of Belleville

front.notes: Decorative fence (front-yard ornamental): ≤3 ft tall and ≥25% open.

opacity.notes: Decorative front fence must be ≥25% open (≤75% opaque).

setback.notes: Certified survey + adjacent owner agreement required.

permit.notes: Fence permit required from Building Department. Posthole inspection AND final inspection required. Fence types defined in ordinance: Chain Link / Ornamental / Partition / Decorative.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: City of Belleville — Department of Building and Planning

belleville-city.research_notes: City of Belleville is a small island city within Van Buren Township. ZIP 48111 covers City + Van Buren Twp + Sumpter Twp — three-way jurisdictional confusion. ZO §5.8 governs fences (Clearzoning-format ZO with section numbers similar to City of Brighton's). Floodplain review may apply (city is on Belleville Lake). Recommend a 5-minute call to extract substantive heights for side/rear and any opacity rules.

van-buren-twp.name: Charter Township of Van Buren

front.notes: Not extracted.

permit.notes: Fence permit required from Van Buren Township Building Department. Apply online via BS&A Online. Permit fee not extracted.

pool_barrier.notes: §7.206 governs pool zoning. Building permit required for permanent above/below ground pools and any portable pool over 12 ft diameter or 100 sq ft. Pool building code questions: 734-699-8900 ext. 9219.

permit_office.name: Charter Township of Van Buren — Building Department / Department of Development Services

van-buren-twp.research_notes: §7.205 confirmed via township FAQ. Township uses BS&A Online (consistent with Plymouth, Livonia, Brighton, Canton). CRITICAL: Van Buren Charter Township (this record), City of Belleville, and Sumpter Township share ZIP 48111. Most homes in 48111 are in Van Buren Township by area, but a small island is the City of Belleville and Sumpter Twp is to the south.

sumpter-twp.name: Sumpter Township

ordinance.chapter: Sumpter Township Code Appendix A (Zoning Ordinance, 2013)
ordinance.title: Fences

front.notes: Not extracted.

setback.notes: ZO defines 'Corner Clear Zone' for sight visibility.

permit.notes: Building Permit required for fences (per ZO definition: 'Building Permit' covers construction/removal/moving/alteration of building, fence, or sign). Fee not extracted.

corner_lot.notes: Corner Clear Zone rule applies (no structures, grade changes, or plantings within sight visibility area).

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: Sumpter Township — Planning & Zoning

sumpter-twp.research_notes: ZO is in Municode (Appendix A). ZIP 48111 SHARED with City of Belleville and Van Buren Township — three-way jurisdiction confusion. Sumpter is south of Van Buren, with the City of Belleville island to the north. Sumpter is the southernmost township in Wayne County.

brighton-city.name: City of Brighton

front.notes: Specific front-yard residential height not extracted from §98-5.8 text.

permit.notes: Fence permit required from City of Brighton Building Department. Mech/Plumb/Elec (separate from fence) go through Livingston County Building Department (517-546-3240). Zoning questions: 810-225-9255. Permit questions: 810-225-8019. PENALTY for working without a permit: $200 residential, $400 commercial.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code (2015 MI Residential Code adopted).

permit_office.name: City of Brighton — Community Development / Building Department

brighton-city.research_notes: Confirmed from primary city sources: §98-5.8 is the fence section. City handles zoning + structural building permits. Livingston County (517-546-3240) handles mechanical/plumbing/electrical only — for fences, only city permit needed. DUAL-JURISDICTION pattern (city + county) similar to Dexter. Penalty $200 res / $400 commercial. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: City of Brighton (this record, brightoncitymi.gov) vs. Charter Township of Brighton (separate record, brightontwp.com, Article 13 §13-04 with detailed rules). ZIPs 48114 and 48116 cover both. Recommend a 5-minute call to 810-225-9255 for §98-5.8 substantive heights.

brighton-twp.name: Charter Township of Brighton

front.notes: Front yard: solid fence/wall max 3 ft. Ornamental fence may be up to 6 ft if open-to-solid ratio ≥1:1 (open portion ≥ solid portion).

side.notes: Residential side/rear max 8 ft. On corner lots, no solid fence/wall over 3 ft in side yard from building front line to ROW line.

opacity.notes: Front yard ornamental fence (up to 6 ft): ratio of solid to open ≤1:1 (must be at least 50% open). Solid front fence/wall capped at 3 ft.

setback.notes: Yard requirements increased by 1 ft for each foot of fence height over the maximum permitted (i.e., taller fences require larger setbacks). Sight clearance on corner lots: no obscuring view; no solid hedge over 3 ft on property line where it would restrict sight from drives onto streets/sidewalks. Pools: above-ground 10 ft from side line / 20 ft from rear; in-ground requires barrier ≥4 ft, openings ≤4", #9 gauge metal/wire.

permit.notes: Permit required. Building Department reviews. Fee not extracted; recommend confirming.

corner_lot.notes: Corner lot side yard: no wall/fence/sign/structure that may cause traffic danger by obscuring view. No solid fence/wall over 3 ft from front building line to ROW line in side yard on corner.

pool_barrier.notes: Above-ground temp pools: side ≥10 ft / rear ≥20 ft setback. In-ground: full barrier required, ≥4 ft, openings ≤4", finished side facing out, #9 gauge metal/wire.

materials.notes: Finished side of fence must face out (toward adjacent property or street), with posts to the inside.

permit_office.name: Charter Township of Brighton — Building Department

brighton-twp.research_notes: VERIFIED from primary township source: Article 13 §13-04 of the township ZO (PDF on township site). Heights and ornamental-fence ratio rule extracted directly. The 8-ft residential maximum is unusually generous compared to neighboring jurisdictions (most cap at 6 ft). The ornamental-fence ratio rule allows visibility through taller fences in front yards (similar in spirit to Putnam Township's 'open fence ≤20% solid' rule). Pool barrier rules captured directly from §13-13. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: Charter Township of Brighton (this record, brightontwp.com) vs. City of Brighton (separate record, brightoncitymi.gov, eCode Chapter 98). Both share ZIPs 48114 and 48116 — recurring jurisdiction confusion. Fee and exact setback values not extracted; recommend a 5-minute call to confirm.

genoa-twp.name: Genoa Charter Township

ordinance.title: Fences (definitions in Article 25; fence regulations dispersed across articles)

front.notes: Not extracted.

opacity.notes: ZO defines 'Privacy Fence' as over 90% solid or impervious.

permit.notes: Land Use Permit required for fences, decks, pools, generators ($50 fee). Permit valid for 12 months. Mech/electrical/plumbing/building permits go to Livingston County Building Department (517-546-3240). For a fence project, only the Township Land Use Permit is needed.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool permit required ($50). Specific barrier requirements not extracted.

materials.notes: ZO Article 25 defines fence types including privacy (>90% solid). Retaining walls, seawalls, and landscaping walls are NOT considered fences for ZO purposes.

permit_office.name: Genoa Charter Township — Development and Zoning

genoa-twp.research_notes: ZO uses Article-based numbering (Article 25 = Definitions; fence regulations dispersed). $50 fence permit fee confirmed. DUAL-JURISDICTION pattern with Livingston County (similar to City of Brighton). CRITICAL: Genoa Township in MICHIGAN, Livingston County (NOT the Genoa Township in Ohio's Delaware County, which is also a fence-relevant entity). Genoa MI shares ZIPs 48114, 48116, 48843 with City of Brighton, City of Howell, Charter Township of Brighton, and others.

green-oak-twp.name: Charter Township of Green Oak

front.notes: Not extracted from §38-176.

setback.notes: §38-136 governs schedule of area, height and setback regulations.

permit.notes: Land use permit required per §38-43. Fee not extracted.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: Charter Township of Green Oak — Planning, Zoning, and Building

green-oak-twp.research_notes: §38-176 confirmed via township's own ordinance reference page. Code is in Municode at standard URL. Large rural-suburban township in southern Livingston County containing parts of South Lyon (city) annexation, Whitmore Lake area, and Lakelands subdivisions. ZIP 48116 also covers City of Brighton (different jurisdiction).

chelsea-city.name: City of Chelsea

front.notes: Front yard residential: max 4 ft, 50% opacity max.

rear.notes: Rear yard residential: max 6 ft. Public-facing fences allowed up to 8 ft if 75% visible.

opacity.notes: Front yard: 50% opacity max. Public 8-ft fences require 75% visibility.

setback.notes: Min 1 ft (12 inches) setback from sidewalk/ROW.

permit.notes: City Planning & Zoning issues fence permits (305 S Main St Suite 100, 734-475-1771 opt 5). Building permit (if required) goes through CACA — Chelsea Area Construction Agency, 12172 Jackson Rd, Dexter, 734-475-4420.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: City of Chelsea — Planning and Zoning
permit_office.address: 305 S Main Street Suite 100, Chelsea, MI 48118

chelsea-city.research_notes: ZO §6.08 governs fences. CACA member for building permits. CRITICAL: City of Chelsea is an island within Sylvan Township; ZIP 48118 covers City + Sylvan Twp + Lyndon Twp + Lima Twp + Sharon Twp + Dexter Twp. Verify jurisdiction at Map Washtenaw GIS before quoting numbers. Recommend a 5-minute call to confirm fee.

dexter-twp.name: Dexter Township

front.notes: Not extracted.

permit.notes: DUAL-PERMIT PATHWAY (CACA member): Township issues a Zoning Permit; building permits go through Chelsea Area Construction Agency (CACA), 12172 Jackson Rd, Dexter MI 48130, (734) 475-4420. Final inspection required after fence work complete.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: Dexter Township — Planning and Zoning

dexter-twp.research_notes: ZO 2020 (effective Oct 2020, amended June 2025), Section F 'Fences and Walls'. CACA member for building permits. Office hours 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM, by appointment only. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: Dexter Township (this record, CACA member for building) vs City of Dexter (separate record, uses Washtenaw County NOT CACA) — recurring confusion.

lyndon-twp.name: Lyndon Township

ordinance.chapter: Lyndon Township Zoning Ordinance 26-002 (adopted April 17, 2026 — NEW)
ordinance.title: Fences

front.notes: Not extracted from new Ordinance 26-002. Township provides a Fence Regulations Handout PDF (3-20-2020 version) — recommend downloading and confirming whether handout has been updated for the new 2026 ZO.

permit.notes: DUAL-PERMIT PATHWAY (CACA member): Township issues Zoning Permit; building permits via CACA. Submit electronically to ordinance@lyndontownshipmi.gov.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: Lyndon Township — Planning and Zoning

lyndon-twp.research_notes: Lyndon adopted Ordinance 26-002 (new ZO) on April 17, 2026 — recent change. Township provides a dedicated Fence Regulations Handout PDF (currently dated March 20, 2020 — may be updated). DUAL-PERMIT PATHWAY: CACA member. Office hours TUE/THU 9 AM – 1 PM ONLY.

sylvan-twp.name: Sylvan Township

front.notes: Not extracted.

permit.notes: DUAL-PERMIT PATHWAY (CACA member): Township issues Zoning Compliance Permit; building permits via CACA. Submit electronically to spacheco@sylvantownshipmi.gov.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: Sylvan Township — Planning and Zoning Administrator

sylvan-twp.research_notes: CACA member. Same Ordinance Officer (Scott Pacheco) appears to serve both Lyndon and Sylvan based on email patterns. Sylvan Township SURROUNDS the City of Chelsea — the city is an island within the township.

dexter-city.name: City of Dexter

front.notes: Not extracted from §3.09.

permit.notes: TWO-STEP PATHWAY: (1) Preliminary Zoning Compliance Permit from City of Dexter; (2) Building Permit from Washtenaw County Building Department; (3) Final Zoning Compliance Permit from City of Dexter (required for fences). NOTE: City of Dexter does NOT use CACA — uses Washtenaw County (different from Dexter Township).

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: City of Dexter — Office of Community Development

dexter-city.research_notes: City of Dexter uses TWO-STEP zoning compliance permit (Preliminary + Final). Building permits go to WASHTENAW COUNTY (not CACA, despite ZIP 48130 being CACA territory for Dexter Township). Two contacts: Michelle Aniol (Community Development Manager) 734-580-2233 maniol@dextermi.gov; David Jarvi (Associate Planner) 734-580-2235 djarvi@dextermi.gov. Recommend a 5-minute call to extract substantive heights from §3.09. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: City of Dexter (this record, Washtenaw County for building) vs Dexter Township (separate record, CACA for building). Easy to confuse — same ZIP 48130.

hamburg-twp.name: Hamburg Township

ordinance.chapter: Hamburg Township Zoning Ordinance (codified by Municode)
ordinance.title: Fences

front.notes: Township uses figure-based fence height rules — Figure 1 (4-ft Max) and Figure 2 (6-ft Max) for Standard Lots. Front-yard residential typically 4 ft (Figure 1).

side.notes: Side yard residential typically 6 ft (Figure 2 standard lots).

rear.notes: Rear yard residential typically 6 ft (Figure 2 standard lots). Special figures for corner lots (Figs 3-5), waterfront lots (Fig 6), and through lots (Figs 7-8).

opacity.notes: Specific opacity not extracted; recommend reviewing the township's fence brochure with figures.

setback.notes: Sewer-specific rule: Fence must be 7.5 ft (90 inches) from the grinder pump. Nothing allowed over the sewer lateral line easement. Property line setbacks vary by lot type per figures. Utilities Department contact: Brittany Campbell, 810-222-1193.

permit.notes: Hamburg Township Land Use Permit required. $40 residential fence permit fee. Cash, check ('Hamburg Township'), or debit/credit card (2.8% fee). DUAL-PERMIT PATHWAY: Township land use permit + Livingston County Building Department permit if required by County. Submittals: plot plan, letter of authorization from owner (or signed contract), picture of proposed fence type/style, fee.

corner_lot.notes: Specific rules for corner lots — see Figures 3, 4, and 5 in township brochure.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

materials.notes: Specific prohibited-materials list not extracted from primary source.

permit_office.name: Hamburg Township — Planning and Zoning Department
permit_office.address: 10405 Merrill Road, Hamburg, MI 48139 (mailing: P.O. Box 157, Hamburg, MI 48139)

hamburg-twp.research_notes: VERIFIED from primary township source. Hamburg has a dedicated 'Fences - Documents Needed' page with explicit fee ($40), submittal requirements, sewer-specific setback (7.5 ft from grinder pump), and figure-based height system. Class 8 NFIP CRS community — floodplain_review flagged true (Huron River corridor). Office hours: Mon–Thu 7:30 AM – 5:30 PM. Three staff: David Rohr (Director, 810-222-1167), Ted Michowski (Code Enforcement, 810-222-1168), Lisa Perschke (Coordinator, 810-222-1166).

livonia-city.name: City of Livonia

front.notes: NO front yard fences allowed in residential districts. This is unusual and contractor-relevant — Livonia bans front-yard fencing entirely.

side.notes: Partition fence (open style): 4 ft max. Privacy fence: 6 ft 4 in (6.33 ft) max. Privacy screen: 6 ft 6 in (6.5 ft) max.

rear.notes: Privacy fence 6 ft 4 in max; privacy screen 6 ft 6 in max.

opacity.notes: Partition fence is open/picket style; privacy fence is solid. Privacy screen is a separate category, max 6.5 ft.

setback.notes: Corner clearance: no fence/wall/landscaping/shrubbery above 30" within triangular area at intersections (25 ft along each ROW line from intersection).

permit.notes: Zoning permit required from Inspection Department for erecting, re-erecting, altering, relocating, or making major repairs. Owner OR contractor responsible for permit (whoever is doing the work). Both can be liable for violations. Painting/staining repairs do NOT require a permit. Decorative landscape fences/hedges (sole purpose ornamental) do not require a permit. Apply via BS&A Online (https://bsaonline.com); schedule final inspection ≥1 business day before by 3:30 PM.

corner_lot.notes: 30" max height within 25-ft sight triangle at any intersection.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

prohibited: barbed wire (residential and abutting residential properties)
materials.notes: Barbed wire prohibited between/abutting residential properties or in/along streets. Stranded barbed wire allowed for industrial uses not abutting residential. No fence shall obstruct streets, alleys, sidewalks. HOA approval may be required separately — city does not enforce HOA rules.

permit_office.name: City of Livonia — Inspection Department

livonia-city.research_notes: VERIFIED via Livonia's official Fence Guide PDF. Comprehensive ordinance with three categories: partition fence (open, ≤4 ft), privacy fence (solid, ≤6 ft 4 in), and privacy screen (≤6 ft 6 in). Front-yard fencing is essentially banned in residential — contractors should be aware. Use BS&A Online for permits and inspections. 'Existing fences shall not be enlarged, rebuilt, repaired, or replaced without first having obtained a zoning permit' — minor repairs (paint/stain) excluded. Industrial barbed wire allowed (limited). Final inspection only — no posthole inspection required.

northville-city.name: City of Northville

front.notes: Front yard fences essentially BANNED (exception: lots ≥2 acres + ≥200 ft frontage may have 4-ft front fence).

side.notes: Side yard residential: max 6 ft (per BZA 2017 case).

permit.notes: Permit required. Historic District pre-approval mandatory for properties in HD. BS&A Online used for permits. Building Official Brent Strong (also serves City of Plymouth).

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: City of Northville — Building Department

northville-city.research_notes: City of Northville has a notably restrictive front-yard rule — front fences essentially banned except on very large lots. Historic District restrictions are significant (much of downtown). Same Building Official as City of Plymouth (Brent Strong). Uses BS&A Online for permits. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: City of Northville vs. Charter Township of Northville share ZIPs 48167 and 48168.

northville-twp.name: Charter Township of Northville

front.notes: Residential ≤2-acre lots: front yard fence max 4 ft, with decorative projections allowed up to 54 inches total. Vision must not be obstructed by more than 25%, with open areas evenly distributed. Fences NOT permitted within the required front yard setback (must be at or behind front building line).

side.notes: Residential side yard: max 6 ft. Vision must not be obstructed by more than 50%.

rear.notes: Residential rear yard: max 6 ft. Vision not obstructed by more than 50%.

opacity.notes: Residential front yard: vision not obstructed by more than 25% (75% open). Residential side/rear: vision not obstructed by more than 50% (50% opacity max). Privacy screens (>50% opacity): max 5 ft, max 75% obstruction.

setback.notes: Residential front yard fences not permitted within the required front yard setback. Pool barriers required for any facility containing more than 24 inches of water at any location.

permit.notes: Three-tier permit system: (1) Fences ≤36 inches: NO permit required if used as decorative landscape feature, sections ≤20 ft long, sections ≥10 ft apart, total length ≤100 ft. (2) Fences >36 inches and ≤7 ft: zoning compliance permit required. (3) Fences >7 ft: building permit required. Finished side must abut adjacent lot/road right-of-way/easement.

corner_lot.notes: Standard corner-lot/sight-triangle rules apply. Township explicitly notes HOAs in Northville often have more stringent rules than the township.

pool_barrier.notes: Required for any pool/spa/hot tub containing more than 24 inches of water. Pool itself must be set back min 10 ft from any property line. Decks/walks around pool min 10 ft from any property line. Mechanical equipment min 5 ft from any property line. Min 10-ft separation between facility and principal structure. Enclosure must be approved by Building Official.

prohibited: chain-link except in Consumer Industrial district vinyl-coated black or dark green
materials.notes: Barbed wire and electric current prohibited unless otherwise specified. Chain-link only allowed in Consumer Industrial district and only if vinyl-coated black or dark green. Finished side must abut adjacent lot/ROW/easement.

permit_office.name: Charter Township of Northville — Building / Planning Department

northville-twp.research_notes: VERIFIED via ecode360 §170-31.5. Three-tier permit system (no permit / zoning compliance / building permit) is contractor-friendly. The 25%-vision-obstruction rule for residential front yards is unusual; the equivalent 'opacity' translation is 75% open / 25% opaque (much more open than the typical 50/50 picket-style). Privacy screens up to 5 ft and 75% obstruction allowed within yard areas (separate from perimeter fences). Township explicitly says many HOAs are MORE stringent than the township's rules. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: Charter Township of Northville (this record) and City of Northville are SEPARATE jurisdictions sharing ZIPs 48167 and 48168.

salem-twp.name: Salem Township

front.notes: Not extracted.

permit.notes: Permit required for all new structures. Salem Township has its own Building Department (NOT CACA, NOT Washtenaw County). Building Coordinator Anne E. Alexander, 248-912-6720. Inspections handled by independent contractor inspectors — set by calling Building Department by 4 PM the day prior. Building inspections Mon–Thu 8am–4pm; Electrical/Mechanical/Plumbing Tue+Thu 8am–4pm.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: Salem Township — Building Department

salem-twp.research_notes: Salem Township in WASHTENAW County (NOT Allegan County's Salem Township at salemtownship.org). Township at salem-mi.org. Building Coordinator Anne E. Alexander, 248-912-6720. NOTE: Salem uses 248 area code despite being in Washtenaw County (border township with Wayne and Oakland Counties — South Lyon/Northville area). ZIP overlap: 48167, 48170, 48178.

pinckney-village.name: Village of Pinckney

front.notes: Not extracted.

permit.notes: Land Use Permit required for most construction including decks, fences, additions, and pools. CRITICAL: Livingston County Building Department will NOT issue their required permits without a Village land use permit first. Two-step pathway: (1) Village Land Use Permit; (2) Livingston County Building Permit. Zoning Administrator reachable through Village Hall by appointment, or zoning@villageofpinckney.org.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: Village of Pinckney — Zoning Department

pinckney-village.research_notes: Village of Pinckney is an incorporated village WITHIN Putnam Township (similar Village-within-Township pattern). DUAL-PERMIT PATHWAY: Village Land Use Permit FIRST, then Livingston County Building Permit. County will NOT issue building permits without Village permit first. Projects must comply with Village ZO AND Technical Standards for Design & Construction. Zoning Administrator reachable by appointment only. Village shares ZIP 48169 with Putnam Twp, Hamburg Twp, and other Pinckney-area jurisdictions — verify jurisdiction before quoting numbers.

putnam-twp.name: Putnam Township

ordinance.chapter: Township Code Chapter 340 (Zoning), Article III General Provisions §340-12 (Fences)
ordinance.title: Fences

front.notes: Residential: SOLID fences in required front yard max 3 ft. Open fence (≤20% solid) may be permitted in front setback area, max 4 ft.

opacity.notes: Front-yard solid fence (100% opaque): 3-ft max. Front-yard 'open' fence (≤20% solid, i.e., ≥80% open): 4-ft max. In LR-1 and LR-2 (Lake Residential) districts, no solid fence (>40% opaque) in waterfront yard or any side yard adjacent to waterfront yard may exceed 3.5 ft.

setback.notes: Setback rules not extracted in detail, but 'required front yard' is a reference point.

permit.notes: DUAL-PERMIT PATHWAY: Putnam Township Zoning Compliance Permit required FIRST. Then Livingston County Building Department issues building permit (when required). County will not issue permits without township zoning compliance. Apply via zoningadmin@putnamtwp.us.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

prohibited: plastic inserts within a chain-link fence
materials.notes: Fences must be constructed of durable materials: brick, stone, decorative concrete, metal, extruded vinyl, or decay-resistant wood. Plastic inserts in chain-link fence explicitly PROHIBITED. Protective fences required by state/federal/township codes (e.g., construction sites) permitted in all districts.

permit_office.name: Putnam Township — Zoning Department

putnam-twp.research_notes: VERIFIED from primary source (ecode360 §340-12). The 'open fence ≤20% solid' rule allows higher front-yard fences if effectively transparent — picket-style or wrought-iron — useful for fence contractors recommending styles. Lake Residential districts have a specific 3.5-ft cap on solid fences in waterfront yards, addressing lake-view preservation. Plastic chain-link inserts explicitly prohibited (unique provision). DUAL-PERMIT PATHWAY: Township + Livingston County. Putnam Township SURROUNDS Pinckney Village (similar Township-around-Village pattern as Sylvan-around-Chelsea).

plymouth-city.name: City of Plymouth

ordinance.chapter: Code of Ordinances Chapter 18 Article X §18-380
ordinance.title: Fences

front.notes: Front yard fence max 30 inches (2.5 ft). Corner lots may have two front yards depending on adjacent house orientation.

side.notes: Side yard residential: max 6.5 ft. Privacy max 6.5 ft; non-privacy max 4 ft. Adjacent to commercial property: up to 8 ft may be allowed.

rear.notes: Rear yard residential: max 6.5 ft. Same materials/style limits as side yard.

opacity.notes: Wood/vinyl picket fences must use 1"x3" picket with 3" picket spacing and ≥90-degree picket top.

setback.notes: Fence must be on applicant's property. May be placed in vacated alley if included in legal description. Clear vision area: no fence over 30" within 25 ft of street corner or 15 ft of driveway/alley. Corner lots may extend into side yard, no closer than 3 ft from side property line, not extending beyond rear face of house.

permit.notes: Permit fee $75 ($60 permit + $15 plan review). Fence repairs require a permit just like new installs. Grandfathered fences lose grandfathered status if you apply for a repair permit. Materials must withstand rust/deterioration ≥10 years.

corner_lot.notes: Corner lots may have two front yards. Sight clearance: 30" max within 25 ft of intersection, 15 ft of driveway.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barrier required per Michigan Residential Code.

materials.notes: Approved styles: vinyl, wood, chain link, aluminum, composite. Privacy, picket, ornamental allowed. Finished side must face neighbor (posts on owner's side); double-sided fences exempt.

permit_office.name: City of Plymouth — Community Development
permit_office.address: 201 S Main Street, 2nd Floor, Plymouth, MI 48170

plymouth-city.research_notes: VERIFIED from primary sources including the City of Plymouth FAQ page and Plymouth's official fence permit application PDF. Permit fee structure ($60 + $15 plan review = $75 total) confirmed by multiple sources including a fence-contractor reference. Building Official Brent Strong reachable at ext. 229. Code Enforcement at ext. 231. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: City of Plymouth (this record) and Plymouth Charter Township (separate jurisdiction, Ord. 99) share ZIP 48170. City has 3- and 4-digit street addresses only; Township has longer addresses. Verify at bsaonline.com if uncertain.

plymouth-twp.name: Charter Township of Plymouth

front.notes: Front yard 6-ft privacy fences are NOT allowed by right (variance required). Specific allowed front-yard heights not extracted.

side.notes: Not extracted; ZBA approved a 6-ft side privacy fence variance in 2021 suggesting 6 ft may be at or near by-right limit.

permit.notes: Fence permit required. Apply via the township's fence permit application PDF. Fee not located in source materials. Finished side of fence must face neighbor.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

permit_office.name: Charter Township of Plymouth — Community Development

plymouth-twp.research_notes: Plymouth Twp is governed by Zoning Ordinance No. 99 (separate from American Legal Publishing Code of Ordinances). Article 6 Sec (f)(3)(c) is the residential fence section, last amended 4/9/2019. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: City of Plymouth (Ch 18 Article X §18-380, $75 permit) vs. Plymouth Charter Township (Ord. 99) share ZIP 48170. City has 3- and 4-digit street addresses only; Township has longer addresses.

canton-twp.name: Charter Township of Canton

ordinance.chapter: Township Code of Ordinances Article 1.00 and 2.00
ordinance.title: Fences

front.notes: Front yard fences may extend up to the front setback line if they do not exceed 4 feet in height. NOTE: ordinance also states 'No fence or wall is allowed in the front yard setback' — these two rules need on-call clarification (likely the 4-ft fence is allowed in the front yard but not within the setback area between front lot line and front of house).

side.notes: Side yard max 4 ft EXCEPT if the fence does not extend closer to the front lot line than 15 feet behind the front corner of the principal building on that side, in which case it can be up to 6 feet high.

opacity.notes: Specific opacity rules not extracted; mesh/vinyl-slat add-ons explicitly prohibited.

setback.notes: Fence including footings/posts must be on owner's property unless on shared line with written neighbor consent. Property line construction requires Consent to Construct form. Area between fence and lot line must be maintained by owner.

permit.notes: Fence permit required for all new fences and panel replacements. Repairs to a panel or post do not require a permit. Minimum $40 administration fee + permit fees due when permit is approved. Submit completed application + survey (mortgage survey or plot plan) + neighbor authorization (if shared line) + manufacturer specs. Final fence inspection required, scheduled via BS&A Online (https://bsaonline.com). Fence posts must be installed minimum 36 inches below grade.

corner_lot.notes: See ordinance for corner-lot rules; CityView Portal at https://emsd.canton-mi.org/cityviewportal lets users verify zoning district.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

prohibited: mesh, vinyl slats, or similar material attached to a fence (privacy add-ons)
materials.notes: Privacy add-ons attached to a fence (mesh, vinyl slats, etc.) explicitly prohibited. Fence must have finished appearance from both sides; if construction has a more-finished side, that side must face exterior of lot.

permit_office.name: Charter Township of Canton — Department of Municipal Services, Building and Inspection Services
permit_office.address: 1150 S. Canton Center Road, Canton, MI 48188

canton-twp.research_notes: VERIFIED from primary source: Canton's official Fence Permit Application PDF includes a 'Residential Fence Ordinance Summary' covering all key fields. The 15-ft side-yard rule is unusual: side fences capped at 4 ft EXCEPT in the rear-of-the-house portion (15 ft behind front corner of principal building), where 6 ft is allowed. Privacy add-ons (mesh, slats) explicitly prohibited. Canton uses BS&A Online for inspections. HOA approval not required for permit BUT township explicitly disclaims liability — applicant must check HOA before applying. 36-inch post-depth requirement explicitly stated. Inspection requirement: applicants must mark property corners before final inspection.

saline-city.name: City of Saline

front.notes: Front yard typically ~36 inches ornamental (regional pattern). Specific city rule not extracted; recommend confirming.

side.notes: Side yard residential typically 6 ft. Specific city rule not extracted.

rear.notes: Rear yard residential typically 6 ft. Specific city rule not extracted.

permit.notes: Permit required from Saline Building Department. Fee not extracted.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

materials.notes: Standard residential prohibitions on barbed/spiked/electric materials likely apply.

permit_office.name: City of Saline — Building Department

ypsilanti-city.name: City of Ypsilanti

front.notes: Front yard residential: max 4 ft, 50% opacity max. Confirmed via R-1 New Construction Fact Sheet.

side.notes: Side yard residential: max 6 ft, no opacity restriction.

rear.notes: Rear yard residential: max 6 ft, no opacity restriction. Street side yard: max 6 ft, no opacity restriction.

opacity.notes: Front yard: 50% opacity max. Side, street side, and rear: no opacity restriction. From R-1 New Construction Fact Sheet.

permit.notes: Zoning Review Application required for fences (specifically named in city's Permit Center forms list). Building Department: One South Huron St 3rd Floor, 734-482-1025. Code Enforcement violations: 734-482-9626. Fee not located in available materials.

corner_lot.notes: Corner lots not specifically addressed in available materials.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

materials.notes: Specific prohibited-materials list not extracted from primary source.

permit_office.name: City of Ypsilanti — Building Department
permit_office.address: One South Huron Street, 3rd Floor, Ypsilanti, MI 48197

ypsilanti-city.research_notes: Heights upgraded to verified via R-1 New Construction Fact Sheet, which explicitly states front 4 ft / 50% opaque, side/street side/rear 6 ft no opacity restriction. ZO citation §122-635 confirmed via Municode. Apply via Zoning Review Application (city explicitly lists this for fences/sheds/etc on Permit Center page). Historic District restrictions apply in much of central Ypsilanti — recommend confirming whether any specific job is in the historic overlay before quoting. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: City of Ypsilanti vs. Charter Township of Ypsilanti share ZIPs 48197/48198.

ypsilanti-twp.name: Charter Township of Ypsilanti

front.notes: Residential: Only ornamental-type fences allowed in required front yard, max 4 ft. Per Ord. 2023-505 amendment, 6-ft fence in secondary front yard (through lots) requires 10-ft setback from front lot line.

side.notes: Residential: 6 ft max in side yard.

rear.notes: Residential: 6 ft max in rear yard.

opacity.notes: Front yard fences must be 'ornamental type' (definition in §1305 not extracted).

setback.notes: Through lots (frontage on two parallel streets): 4-6 ft fences in secondary front yard require 10 ft setback. Fence over 6 ft must meet 35-ft rear yard setback.

permit.notes: Zoning Permit required for all residential fences (separate from Building Permit). Smooth/finished side of fence must face out (unless shadow-box style). Property survey OR notarized written consent from adjacent neighbors required if fence is on property line. Non-residential/commercial fence requires a Building Permit. Phone (734) 544-4000 ext. 1. Fee not extracted.

corner_lot.notes: Through-lot/corner-lot rules in §1305 — 10 ft setback for taller fences in secondary front yard.

pool_barrier.notes: Pool barriers per Michigan Residential Code.

materials.notes: Specific prohibited materials in §1305 not extracted from primary source.

permit_office.name: Charter Township of Ypsilanti — Planning & Zoning
permit_office.address: 7200 S Huron River Drive, Ypsilanti, MI 48197

ypsilanti-twp.research_notes: Primary source: Charter Township of Ypsilanti Zoning Ordinance §1305, as amended by Ord. 2023-505 (adopted September 19, 2023). Heights confirmed from 2023-505 amendment text. Permit pathway: Zoning Permit for residential fences (NOT a building permit), Building Permit for non-residential fences. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: Charter Township of Ypsilanti (this record) and City of Ypsilanti are SEPARATE jurisdictions sharing ZIP codes 48197/48198. City uses Chapter 122 §122-635; Township uses §1305. Verify jurisdiction at Map Washtenaw GIS before quoting numbers.
